original_hypothesis.title: UNIv3 TVL Overcounting via Aggregate Pool Position Query Enables Share Price Inflation
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: UNIv3Connector._getPositionTVL queries pool.positions(key) using keccak256(positionManager, tickLower, tickUpper), which returns the AGGREGATE liquidity for ALL NonfungiblePositionManager NFTs at those ticks, not just the vault's specific tokenId. Any external user's liquidity at the same tick range through the same positionManager inflates the vault's reported TVL. An attacker can deposit into the vault, add external liquidity at the same tick range, inflate TVL, then withdraw vault shares at the inflated price.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The pool.positions(key) aggregation is a fundamental accounting error. The correct approach uses positionManager.positions(tokenId) for per-NFT liquidity. Since Uniswap V3 positions at popular tick ranges are common, this is always exploitable.

proof_of_signal.pattern: pool.positions(keccak256(positionManager, tL, tU)) returns aggregate liquidity, not per-tokenId liquidity

preconditions: Vault has at least one UNIv3 LP position
Attacker can add liquidity to same pool/tick range via the same NonfungiblePositionManager
AccountingManager uses TVL for share pricing

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: pool.positions(key) returns ALL liquidity from positionManager at ticks, not just vault's tokenId
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker adds external liquidity at same tick range to inflate vault TVL

economic_questions: What is the typical ratio of vault liquidity to total liquidity at a tick range?
How much capital is needed to significantly inflate the TVL?
Can the attack be done atomically with a flash loan?
validation_requirements: Verify pool.positions(key) vs positionManager.positions(tokenId) output difference
Calculate capital needed to double apparent TVL
Test end-to-end: deposit, inflate, withdraw cycle

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Equal to vault's UNI position size to 2x inflate (flash loanable)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 50% of vault TVL in extreme case
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: High - attacker extracts from other depositors

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Flash loan for same-asset liquidity provision
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: When vault has concentrated UNI v3 positions
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Low price impact - just adding/removing liquidity

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Pro-rata loss proportional to TVL inflation

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Quantify overcounting with real pool data

validation_result.mechanism: Aggregate pool position query instead of per-NFT liquidity lookup
validation_result.impact_summary: UNIv3Connector._getPositionTVL queries aggregate pool-level position (all NonfungiblePositionManager NFTs at same ticks) instead of per-tokenId liquidity, inflating vault TVL and enabling share price manipulation.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The _getPositionTVL function in UNIv3Connector.sol decodes the tokenId from the holding position data but never uses it. Instead, it constructs a key using keccak256(abi.encodePacked(positionManager, tL, tU)) and queries pool.positions(key). In Uniswap V3's pool contract, positions are keyed by keccak256(abi.encodePacked(owner, tickLower, tickUpper)). Since the NonfungiblePositionManager is the 'owner' of all positions it manages on behalf of users, this query returns the AGGREGATE liquidity from ALL NFTs minted through that positionManager at the same tick range — not just the vault's specific tokenId. The correct approach is to use positionManager.positions(tokenId) which returns the specific liquidity for a single NFT position. This is a clear code defect: tokenId is extracted but unused, and the wrong data source is queried.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is clearly visible in UNIv3Connector.sol _getPositionTVL function. Line 'uint256 tokenId = abi.decode(p.data, (uint256));' extracts the tokenId but it is never referenced again. Instead, 'bytes32 key = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(positionManager, tL, tU));' and '(uint128 liquidity,,, uint128 tokensOwed0, uint128 tokensOwed1) = pool.positions(key);' query the aggregate pool-level position. The liquidity returned includes contributions from ALL positions through the same positionManager at those ticks.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The tokenId is decoded from p.data but never used for the TVL calculation. Instead, the function queries pool.positions() with a key derived from the positionManager address and tick bounds. In Uniswap V3, the NonfungiblePositionManager is the 'owner' in the pool's position mapping for ALL NFTs it manages. Therefore pool.positions(keccak256(positionManager, tL, tU)) returns the total aggregate liquidity from every NFT at those ticks — not just the vault's tokenId. The correct fix is to use positionManager.positions(tokenId) to get per-NFT liquidity.

calling_sequence: TVLHelper.getTVL() iterates over all holding positions
For each UNI v3 position, calls IConnector(calculatorConnector).getPositionTVL(position, baseToken)
Queries pool.positions(key) which returns AGGREGATE liquidity for all NFTs at those ticks
Computes token amounts from aggregate liquidity using LiquidityAmounts.getAmountsForLiquidity
Converts to base token value using valueOracle
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The vulnerability is in the TVL calculation path. When TVLHelper.getTVL is called (used by AccountingManager for share pricing), it calls _getPositionTVL for each UNI v3 holding position. The function queries pool.positions(key) with a key that aggregates ALL NonfungiblePositionManager positions at the same tick range, not just the vault's specific tokenId. Any external user's liquidity at the same tick range inflates the vault's reported TVL. This directly affects share pricing: inflated TVL means shares are overvalued, enabling extraction of value from other depositors.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: The deployment script deployUniswapV3Connector.ts shows the connector is deployed with standard Uniswap V3 positionManager and factory addresses, confirming the connector interacts with the real Uniswap V3 protocol where the aggregate position behavior is well-documented.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack scenario is valid: 1) Attacker deposits into the vault to receive shares. 2) Vault has a UNI v3 position at tick range [tL, tU]. 3) Attacker (or any third party) mints a new position via the same NonfungiblePositionManager at the exact same tick range [tL, tU], adding significant liquidity. 4) Vault's TVL is now inflated because _getPositionTVL counts the aggregate liquidity. 5) Attacker withdraws shares at the inflated TVL, extracting value proportional to (inflatedTVL - trueTVL) * (attackerShares / totalShares). The attack can be enhanced with flash loans to temporarily add and remove external liquidity. This is feasible even without an intentional attack — any organic liquidity at the same tick range from other users would passively inflate the vault's TVL.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL. The defect causes the vault to report inflated TVL whenever external liquidity exists at the same tick range through the same NonfungiblePositionManager. This directly affects share pricing, enabling: (1) Existing depositors to withdraw more than their fair share, (2) Attackers to deliberately inflate TVL for profit extraction, (3) Incorrect management and performance fee calculations. All vault depositors are at risk of fund loss.
recommended_mitigations: Replace pool.positions(key) with positionManager.positions(tokenId) to get per-NFT liquidity. The tokenId is already decoded from p.data but unused.
Specifically: call (,,,,,, uint128 liquidity,,,,, ) = positionManager.positions(tokenId) and use that liquidity value instead of the aggregate pool position liquidity.
For owed fees, use the fee growth tracking from positionManager.positions(tokenId) rather than the aggregate tokensOwed0/tokensOwed1 from pool.positions().
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a UNIv3Connector with a real Uniswap V3 pool. 2. Open a position at tick range [tL, tU] via openPosition. 3. Record TVL via _getPositionTVL. 4. From an external account, mint a new position through the same NonfungiblePositionManager at exactly [tL, tU] with equal liquidity. 5. Re-query _getPositionTVL — TVL should approximately double, confirming the overcounting. 6. Remove the external position and verify TVL returns to original value.
uncertainties: The exact magnitude of overcounting depends on how much external liquidity exists at the same tick range, which varies by pool and tick range popularity.
The slot0() spot price usage (separate from this bug) adds additional manipulation surface via flash loans.
additional_testing_needed: End-to-end test with deposit/inflate/withdraw cycle to measure exact profit extraction
Test with popular tick ranges (e.g., full-range positions) where many external positions overlap
Verify that PancakeswapConnector (which inherits UNIv3Connector) has the same vulnerability
